### F40799 CX32: Configured transfer end time exceeded

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
-

**Message class:**
  
Internal (DRIVE-CLiQ) communication error (12)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The configured transfer end time when transferring the cyclic actual values was exceeded.

**Remedy:**
  
- Carry out a POWER ON (switch-off/switch-on) for all components.  
- Contact Technical Support.

### F40801 CX32 DRIVE-CLiQ: Sign-of-life missing

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
Component number: %1, fault cause: %2

**Message class:**
  
Internal (DRIVE-CLiQ) communication error (12)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** Control Unit (CU) | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
Vector: OFF2  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
A DRIVE-CLiQ communications error has occurred from the Control Unit to the controller
extension involved.  
Fault cause:  
10 (= 0A hex):  
The sign-of-life bit in the receive telegram is not set.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
0000yyxx hex: yy = component number, xx = error cause

**Remedy:**
  
- Carry out a POWER ON (switch-off/switch-on).  
- Replace the component involved.

### F40820 CX32 DRIVE-CLiQ: Telegram error

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
Component number: %1, fault cause: %2

**Message class:**
  
Internal (DRIVE-CLiQ) communication error (12)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** DRIVE-CLiQ Hub Module (Hub) | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
Vector: OFF2  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
A DRIVE-CLiQ communications error has occurred from the Control Unit to the controller
extension involved.  
Fault cause:  
1 (= 01 hex):  
Checksum error (CRC error).  
2 (= 02 hex):  
Telegram is shorter than specified in the length byte or in the receive list.  
3 (= 03 hex):  
Telegram is longer than specified in the length byte or in the receive list.  
4 (= 04 hex):  
The length of the receive telegram does not match the receive list.  
5 (= 05 hex):  
The type of the receive telegram does not match the receive list.  
6 (= 06 hex):  
The address of the component in the telegram and in the receive list do not match.  
7 (= 07 hex):  
A SYNC telegram is expected - but the received telegram is not a SYNC telegram.  
8 (= 08 hex):  
No SYNC telegram is expected - but the received telegram is one.  
9 (= 09 hex):  
The error bit in the receive telegram is set.  
16 (= 10 hex):  
The receive telegram is too early.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
0000yyxx hex: yy = component number, xx = error cause

**Remedy:**
  
- Carry out a POWER ON (switch-off/switch-on).  
- Check the electrical cabinet design and cable routing for EMC compliance.  
- Check the DRIVE-CLiQ wiring (interrupted cable, contacts, ...).

### F40825 CX32 DRIVE-CLiQ: Supply voltage failed

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Message value:**
  
Component number: %1, fault cause: %2

**Message class:**
  
Supply voltage fault (undervoltage) (3)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** DRIVE-CLiQ Hub Module (Hub) | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
Vector: OFF1 (OFF2)  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: OFF1 (OFF2)

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The DRIVE-CLiQ communication from the DRIVE-CLiQ component involved to the Control
Unit signals that the supply voltage has failed.  
Fault cause:  
9 (= 09 hex):  
The power supply voltage for the components has failed.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
0000yyxx hex: yy = component number, xx = error cause

**Remedy:**
  
- Carry out a POWER ON (switch-off/switch-on).  
- Check the supply voltage wiring of the DRIVE-CLiQ component (interrupted cable,
contacts, ...).  
- Check the dimensioning of the DRIVE-CLiQ component power supply.

All objects

4.4

extension involved. The nodes do not send and receive in synchronism.

34 (= 22 hex):

Timeout in the telegram send list.  
Note regarding the message value:

- Replace the component involved.

### F40837 CX32 DRIVE-CLiQ: Component fault

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
Component number: %1, fault cause: %2

**Message class:**
  
Internal (DRIVE-CLiQ) communication error (12)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** DRIVE-CLiQ Hub Module (Hub) | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
Vector: OFF2  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
Fault detected on the DRIVE-CLiQ component concerned. Faulty hardware cannot be excluded.  
Fault cause:  
32 (= 20 hex):  
Error in the telegram header.  
35 (= 23 hex):  
Receive error: The telegram buffer memory contains an error.  
66 (= 42 hex):  
Send error: The telegram buffer memory contains an error.  
67 (= 43 hex):  
Send error: The telegram buffer memory contains an error.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
0000yyxx hex: yy = component number, xx = error cause

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the DRIVE-CLiQ wiring (interrupted cable, contacts, ...).  
- Check the electrical cabinet design and cable routing for EMC compliance.  
- If required, use another DRIVE-CLiQ socket (p9904).  
- Replace the component involved.

### F40845 CX32 DRIVE-CLiQ: Cyclic data transfer error

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
Component number: %1, fault cause: %2

**Message class:**
  
Internal (DRIVE-CLiQ) communication error (12)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** DRIVE-CLiQ Hub Module (Hub) | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
Vector: OFF2  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
A DRIVE-CLiQ communications error has occurred from the Control Unit to the controller
extension involved.  
Fault cause:  
11 (= 0B hex):  
Synchronization error during alternating cyclic data transfer.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
0000yyxx hex: yy = component number, xx = error cause

**Remedy:**
  
Carry out a POWER ON (switch-off/switch-on).

### F40851 CX32 DRIVE-CLiQ (CU): Sign-of-life missing

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
Component number: %1, fault cause: %2

**Message class:**
  
Internal (DRIVE-CLiQ) communication error (12)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** DRIVE-CLiQ Hub Module (Hub) | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
Vector: OFF2  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
A DRIVE-CLiQ communications error has occurred from the controller extension involved
to the Control Unit.  
The DRIVE-CLiQ component did not set the sign-of-life to the Control Unit.  
Fault cause:  
10 (= 0A hex):  
The sign-of-life bit in the receive telegram is not set.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
0000yyxx hex: yy = component number, xx = error cause

**Remedy:**
  
Upgrade the firmware of the component involved.

### F40860 CX32 DRIVE-CLiQ (CU): Telegram error

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
Component number: %1, fault cause: %2

**Message class:**
  
Internal (DRIVE-CLiQ) communication error (12)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** DRIVE-CLiQ Hub Module (Hub) | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
Vector: OFF2  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
A DRIVE-CLiQ communications error has occurred from the controller extension involved
to the Control Unit.  
Fault cause:  
1 (= 01 hex):  
Checksum error (CRC error).  
2 (= 02 hex):  
Telegram is shorter than specified in the length byte or in the receive list.  
3 (= 03 hex):  
Telegram is longer than specified in the length byte or in the receive list.  
4 (= 04 hex):  
The length of the receive telegram does not match the receive list.  
5 (= 05 hex):  
The type of the receive telegram does not match the receive list.  
6 (= 06 hex):  
The address of the power unit in the telegram and in the receive list do not match.  
9 (= 09 hex):  
The error bit in the receive telegram is set.  
16 (= 10 hex):  
The receive telegram is too early.  
17 (= 11 hex):  
CRC error and the receive telegram is too early.  
18 (= 12 hex):  
The telegram is shorter than that specified in the length byte or in the receive list
and the receive telegram is too early.  
19 (= 13 hex):  
The telegram is longer than that specified in the length byte or in the receive list
and the receive telegram is too early.  
20 (= 14 hex):  
The length of the receive telegram does not match the receive list and the receive
telegram is too early.  
21 (= 15 hex):  
The type of the receive telegram does not match the receive list and the receive telegram
is too early.  
22 (= 16 hex):  
The address of the power unit in the telegram and in the receive list does not match
and the receive telegram is too early.  
25 (= 19 hex):  
The error bit in the receive telegram is set and the receive telegram is too early.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
0000yyxx hex: yy = component number, xx = error cause

**Remedy:**
  
- Carry out a POWER ON (switch-off/switch-on).  
- Check the electrical cabinet design and cable routing for EMC compliance.  
- Check the DRIVE-CLiQ wiring (interrupted cable, contacts, ...).

### F40875 CX32 DRIVE-CLiQ (CU): Supply voltage failed

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Message value:**
  
Component number: %1, fault cause: %2

**Message class:**
  
Supply voltage fault (undervoltage) (3)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** DRIVE-CLiQ Hub Module (Hub) | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
Vector: OFF1 (OFF2)  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: OFF1 (OFF2)

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The DRIVE-CLiQ communication from the DRIVE-CLiQ component involved to the Control
Unit signals that the supply voltage has failed.  
Fault cause:  
9 (= 09 hex):  
The power supply voltage for the components has failed.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
0000yyxx hex: yy = component number, xx = error cause

**Remedy:**
  
- Carry out a POWER ON (switch-off/switch-on).  
- Check the supply voltage wiring of the DRIVE-CLiQ component (interrupted cable,
contacts, ...).  
- Check the dimensioning of the DRIVE-CLiQ component power supply.

### F40885 CX32 DRIVE-CLiQ (CU): Cyclic data transfer error

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
Component number: %1, fault cause: %2

**Message class:**
  
Internal (DRIVE-CLiQ) communication error (12)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** DRIVE-CLiQ Hub Module (Hub) | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
Vector: OFF2  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
A DRIVE-CLiQ communications error has occurred from the controller extension involved
to the Control Unit.  
The nodes do not send and receive in synchronism.  
Fault cause:  
26 (= 1A hex):  
Sign-of-life bit in the receive telegram not set and the receive telegram is too early.  
33 (= 21 hex):  
The cyclic telegram has not been received.  
34 (= 22 hex):  
Timeout in the telegram receive list.  
64 (= 40 hex):  
Timeout in the telegram send list.  
98 (= 62 hex):  
Error at the transition to cyclic operation.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
0000yyxx hex: yy = component number, xx = error cause

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the power supply voltage of the component involved.  
- Carry out a POWER ON (switch-off/switch-on).  
- Replace the component involved.

### F40886 CX32 DRIVE-CLiQ (CU): Error when sending DRIVE-CLiQ data

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
Component number: %1, fault cause: %2

**Message class:**
  
Internal (DRIVE-CLiQ) communication error (12)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** DRIVE-CLiQ Hub Module (Hub) | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
Vector: OFF2  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
A DRIVE-CLiQ communications error has occurred from the controller extension involved
to the Control Unit.  
Data were not able to be sent.  
Fault cause:  
65 (= 41 hex):  
Telegram type does not match send list.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
0000yyxx hex: yy = component number, xx = error cause

**Remedy:**
  
Carry out a POWER ON (switch-off/switch-on).

97 (= 61 hex):

### A49111 Fan monitoring: Differential pressure has fallen below the alarm threshold

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, BMM2C, VECTORM2C

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
During operation, at least one differential pressure actual value has fallen below
the set alarm threshold.  
Operation with full power is no longer guaranteed.  
Fault value (r0949, interpret hexadecimal):  
Bit 0: Differential pressure actual value system 1 left cabinet  
Bit 1: Differential pressure actual value system 1 right cabinet  
Bit 2: Differential pressure actual value system 2 left cabinet  
Bit 3: Differential pressure actual value system 2 right cabinet  
See also: p6790

**Remedy:**
  
Check the fan.

### A49113 (F) Fan monitoring: Feedback signal input fan missing

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, BMM2C, VECTORM2C

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
System: %1, Modbus address%2, fault cause %3

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
At least one feedback signal of one of the fans is missing.  
Alarm value (r2124, interpret hexadecimal):  
Fault cause (hexadecimal coded)  
Bit 0: Converter not ready  
Bit 1: Torque limit (fan impeller blocked)  
Bit 2: Motor clockwise rotation  
Bit 3: No motor connected  
Bit 4: Modbus communication error to converter

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the fan.  
- Check the fan wiring.  
- Check the protection elements.

Reaction upon F:  
 NONE

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY

### A49116 (N) Fan monitoring: Operating hours exceeded

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORMV

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
The set fan operating hours have been exceeded.  
Alarm value (r2124, interpret hexadecimal):  
Bit 4: Main fan.  
Bit 5: Redundant main fan (optional).  
Bit 8: Memory or access problems with the operating hours counter on the Power Stack
Adapter (PSA).  
See also: p0252

**Remedy:**
  
Replace the fan and set the operating hours counter display to zero.  
For memory or access problems, check communications with the PSA.  
See also: p0251

Reaction upon N:  
 NONE

Acknowl. upon N:  
NONE

### A49119 (F) Fan monitoring: protective breaker feedback signals missing

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, BMM2C, VECTORM2C

4.4

**Message value:**
  
Fault cause: %1 bin

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
At least one feedback signal from the protective breaker for the fan cooling is missing.  
Message value (r0949/r2124, interpret hexadecimal):  
Bit 00...05: Indicates the status of parameter p6799.0...5 at the instant that the
message occurs.  
See also: p6798

**Remedy:**
  
Check the feedback signals and the protective breaker.

Reaction upon F:  
Vector: OFF2 (OFF1, OFF3)  
Chopper: OFF2  
Infeed: OFF2 (OFF1)

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY

### A49140 Internal overtemperature alarm

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Power electronics faulted (5)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** Power Module | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
The temperature inside a cabinet has exceeded the alarm threshold.  
- Auxiliary fan defective.  
- Insufficient ventilation.  
- Ambient temperature too high.  
Fault cause:  
01: Temperature in cabinet 1 too high.  
02: Temperature in cabinet 2 too high.  
03: Temperature in cabinet 3 too high.  
04: Temperature in cabinet 4 too high.  
05: Temperature in cabinet 5 too high.  
06: Temperature in cabinet 6 too high.  
07: Temperature in cabinet 7 too high.  
08: Temperature in cabinet 8 too high.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
yyxx dec: yy = power unit data set (PDS), xx = fault cause  
Example:  
Message value (r0949/r2124) = 102 dec  
--> the temperature in cabinet 2 with power unit data set 1 (PDS = 1) is too high.

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the auxiliary fan and if required, replace.  
- Ensure that the air circulates.  
- Check that the ambient temperature is in the permissible range  
See also: p6514 (Auxiliary fan thresholds)

### F49141 (A) Internal overtemperature fault

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Power electronics faulted (5)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** Power Module | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

Chopper: OFF2 (NONE)  
Infeed: OFF2 (NONE, OFF1)

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The temperature inside a cabinet has exceeded the fault threshold.  
- Auxiliary fan defective.  
- Insufficient ventilation.  
- Ambient temperature too high.  
Fault cause:  
01: Temperature in cabinet 1 too high.  
02: Temperature in cabinet 2 too high.  
03: Temperature in cabinet 3 too high.  
04: Temperature in cabinet 4 too high.  
05: Temperature in cabinet 5 too high.  
06: Temperature in cabinet 6 too high.  
07: Temperature in cabinet 7 too high.  
08: Temperature in cabinet 8 too high.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
yyxx dec: yy = power unit data set (PDS), xx = fault cause  
Example:  
Message value (r0949/r2124) = 102 dec  
--> the temperature in cabinet 2 with power unit data set 1 (PDS = 1) is too high.

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the auxiliary fan and if required, replace.  
- Ensure that the air circulates.  
- Check that the ambient temperature is in the permissible range  
See also: r6516 (Auxiliary fan fault threshold display)

Reaction upon A:  
 NONE

Acknowl. upon A:  
NONE

### A49142 Switch on auxiliary fan feedback signal missing

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Power electronics faulted (5)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** Power Module | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
No feedback signal was detected after switching on an auxiliary fan.  
- Auxiliary fan defective.  
- Power supply missing.  
Fault cause:  
Bit 0=1: Cabinet 1 fan 1 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 1=1: Cabinet 1 fan 2 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 2=1: Cabinet 2 fan 1 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 3=1: Cabinet 2 fan 2 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 4=1: Cabinet 3 fan 1 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 5=1: Cabinet 3 fan 2 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 6=1: Cabinet 4 fan 1 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 7=1: Cabinet 4 fan 2 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 8=1: Cabinet 5 fan 1 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 9=1: Cabinet 5 fan 2 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 10=1: Cabinet 6 fan 1 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 11=1: Cabinet 6 fan 2 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 12=1: Cabinet 7 fan 1 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 13=1: Cabinet 7 fan 2 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 14=1: Cabinet 8 fan 1 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Bit 15=1: Cabinet 8 fan 2 feedback signal missing after switch on.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
yyxx dec: yy = power unit data set (PDS), xx = fault cause  
Example:  
Message value (r0949/r2124) = 102 dec  
--> the feedback signal from fan 2 in cabinet 1 with the power unit data set 1 (PDS
= 1) is missing after switch on.

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the auxiliary fan and if required, replace.  
- Check the wiring of the auxiliary fan.  
Note:  
After the fault has been removed (the fan has been replaced), p6517.x must be set
= 0 before the fault can be acknowledged.  
See also: p6517 (Auxiliary fan defective)

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
During operation the feedback signal of an auxiliary fan has failed.  
- Auxiliary fan defective.  
- Power supply missing.  
Fault cause:  
Bit 0=1: Cabinet 1 fan 1 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 1=1: Cabinet 1 fan 2 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 2=1: Cabinet 2 fan 1 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 3=1: Cabinet 2 fan 2 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 4=1: Cabinet 3 fan 1 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 5=1: Cabinet 3 fan 2 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 6=1: Cabinet 4 fan 1 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 7=1: Cabinet 4 fan 2 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 8=1: Cabinet 5 fan 1 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 9=1: Cabinet 5 fan 2 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 10=1: Cabinet 6 fan 1 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 11=1: Cabinet 6 fan 2 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 12=1: Cabinet 7 fan 1 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 13=1: Cabinet 7 fan 2 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 14=1: Cabinet 8 fan 1 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Bit 15=1: Cabinet 8 fan 2 feedback signal failed in operation.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
yyxx dec: yy = power unit data set (PDS), xx = fault cause  
Example:  
Message value (r0949/r2124) = 102 dec  
--> the feedback signal from fan 2 in cabinet 1 with the power unit data set 1 (PDS
= 1) has failed in operation.

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the auxiliary fan and if required, replace.  
- Check the wiring of the auxiliary fan.  
Note:  
After the fault has been removed (the fan has been replaced), p6517.x must be set
= 0 before the fault can be acknowledged.  
See also: p6517 (Auxiliary fan defective)

### F49144 (A) Defective fan

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Power electronics faulted (5)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** Power Module | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
Vector: OFF2 (NONE, OFF1)  
Chopper: OFF2 (NONE)  
Infeed: OFF2 (NONE, OFF1)

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The feedback signals "Fan on" are missing, and one of the following events has occurred:  
- The temperature inside the drive converter has exceeded the permissible temperature
limit.  
- The time that a fan takes to signal that it is not defective has expired (multiple
of p6515[6]).  
Fault cause:  
Bit 0=1: Cabinet 1 fan defective  
Bit 1=1: Cabinet 2 fan defective  
Bit 2=1: Cabinet 3 fan defective  
Bit 3=1: Cabinet 4 fan defective  
Bit 4=1: Cabinet 5 fan defective  
Bit 5=1: Cabinet 6 fan defective  
Bit 6=1: Cabinet 7 fan defective  
Bit 7=1: Cabinet 8 fan defective  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
yyxx dec: yy = power unit data set (PDS), xx = fault cause  
Example:  
Message value (r0949/r2124) = 102 dec  
--> the fan is defective in cabinet 2 with power unit data set 1 (PDS = 1).

**Remedy:**
  
Check the auxiliary fan and if required, replace.  
Note:  
After the fault has been removed (the fan has been replaced), p6517.x must be set
= 0 before the fault can be acknowledged.  
See also: p6517 (Auxiliary fan defective)

Reaction upon A:  
 NONE

Acknowl. upon A:  
NONE

### A49145 Redundant auxiliary fan switched on

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Power electronics faulted (5)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** Power Module | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
The redundant auxiliary fan is necessary and was additionally switched on.  
- Insufficient ventilation.  
- Ambient temperature too high.  
Fault cause:  
Bit 0=1: Cabinet 1 redundant fan switched on.  
Bit 1=1: Cabinet 2 redundant fan switched on.  
Bit 2=1: Cabinet 3 redundant fan switched on.  
Bit 3=1: Cabinet 4 redundant fan switched on.  
Bit 4=1: Cabinet 5 redundant fan switched on.  
Bit 5=1: Cabinet 6 redundant fan switched on.  
Bit 6=1: Cabinet 7 redundant fan switched on.  
Bit 7=1: Cabinet 8 redundant fan switched on.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
yyxx dec: yy = power unit data set (PDS), xx = fault cause  
Example:  
Message value (r0949/r2124) = 102 dec  
--> the redundant fan in cabinet 1 with power unit data set 1 (PDS = 1) has been switched
on.

**Remedy:**
  
Not necessary.  
Observe additional system messages.

### A49146 Heat sink fan has exceeded the maximum operating time

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Power electronics faulted (5)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** Power Module | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
The maximum operating time of at least one auxiliary fan has been exceeded.  
Fault cause:  
Bit 0=1: Cabinet 1 fan 1  
Bit 1=1: Cabinet 1 fan 2  
Bit 2=1: Cabinet 2 fan 1  
Bit 3=1: Cabinet 2 fan 2  
Bit 4=1: Cabinet 3 fan 1  
Bit 5=1: Cabinet 3 fan 2  
Bit 6=1: Cabinet 4 fan 1  
Bit 7=1: Cabinet 4 fan 2  
Bit 8=1: Cabinet 5 fan 1  
Bit 9=1: Cabinet 5 fan 2  
Bit 10=1: Cabinet 6 fan 1  
Bit 11=1: Cabinet 6 fan 2  
Bit 12=1: Cabinet 7 fan 1  
Bit 13=1: Cabinet 7 fan 2  
Bit 14=1: Cabinet 8 fan 1  
Bit 15=1: Cabinet 8 fan 2  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
yyxx dec: yy = power unit data set (PDS), xx = fault cause  
Example:  
Message value (r0949/r2124) = 102 dec  
--> For a redundant fan in cabinet 1 with power unit data set 1 (PDS = 1) the maximum
operating time has been exceeded.  
See also: p17746 (Auxiliary fan maximum operating time), p17750 (Subsystem 1 auxiliary fan operating
hours), p17751 (Subsystem 2 auxiliary fan operating hours), p17752 (Subsystem 3 auxiliary
fan operating hours)

**Remedy:**
  
Replace the fan and reset the display of the corresponding operating hours counter
p17750, p17751 or p17752.  
Check the operating hour counters of the other fans, and when required, replace additional
fan(s).

### F49151 Cooling unit: Conductivity has exceeded the fault threshold

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
-

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The conductivity of the cooling liquid has exceeded the selected fault threshold.  
Note:  
The value for the fault value threshold is taken from the equipment description data.  
See also: p0261, p0262, p0266, r0268

**Remedy:**
  
Check the device to de-ionize the cooling liquid.

### F49153 Cooling unit: Liquid flow too low

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The coolant flow is too low.  
Fault cause:  
1 (= 01 hex):  
The actual flow value (r6034) has fallen below the selected fault threshold.  
Note:  
The value for the fault value threshold for the analog liquid flow monitoring is taken
from the equipment description data.  
2 (= 02 hex):  
The feedback signal used to monitor the flow signals that it is not OK (r6033.4 =
0).  
Note:  
The signal source for the feedback signal is set using binector input p6034[4].  
See also: r6034 (Cooling unit, liquid flow actual value), p6036 (Cooling unit feedback signal
signal source)

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the cooling liquid lines.  
- Check the pumps.  
- Check the BICO interconnection.

### F49154 (A) Cooling unit: Liquid leak is present

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
-

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The liquid leakage monitoring function has responded.  
Caution:  
If this fault is reparameterized as an alarm, then using other monitoring functions
it must be ensured that when cooling water is lost, the drive is switched off!  
See also: r0267 (Cooling unit status word)

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the cooling system for leaks in the cooling circuit.  
- Check the wiring of the input terminal (Terminal Module) used to monitor leaking
fluid.

Reaction upon A:  
 NONE

Acknowl. upon A:  
NONE

### F49156 Cooling unit: Cooling liquid temperature has exceeded the fault threshold

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
-

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The cooling liquid intake temperature has exceeded the specified fault threshold.  
Note:  
The value for the fault value threshold is taken from the equipment description data.

**Remedy:**
  
Check the cooling system and the ambient conditions.

### F49157 Cooling unit: Cooling medium temperature has exceeded fault threshold 2

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
-

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The cooling liquid intake temperature has exceeded the permanently set fault threshold
2.  
The drive converter is switched off. The cooling unit is switched on as the cooling
medium temperature has continued to increase in spite of the fact that the drive converter
has been switched off.  
Note:  
The value for the fault value threshold is taken from the equipment description data.

**Remedy:**
  
Check the thermal load of the drive converter.

### F49158 Cooling unit: Differential pressure has fallen below the fault threshold

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The fault threshold for the differential pressure between the liquid intake and water
outlet was either not reached or undershot.  
The fault value is sent the first time the fault occurs.  
The actual fault value can be output from the ring buffer starting at r7100.  
Fault value (r0949, interpret hexadecimal):  
Bit 0: Subsystem 1  
Bit 1: Subsystem 2

**Remedy:**
  
Check the cooling unit.

### F49159 Cooling unit: Fault signal from control device of the cooling unit

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The control device of the cooling system signals a fault or did not set the feedback
signal "cooling system is switched on" or "cooling system is ready" within the monitoring
time.  
The fault value is sent the first time the fault occurs.  
Fault value (r0949, interpret hexadecimal):  
Bit 0: Feedback signal "cooling unit alarm" present (r0267.4).  
Bit 1: Feedback signal "cooling unit fault" present (r0267.5).  
Bit 2: "Switching on inhibited" signal present.  
Bit 3: Feedback signal "Cooling unit switched on" missing (r0267.16).  
Bit 4: Feedback signal "Cooling unit ready" missing (r0267.17).  
Bit 5: "Internal switch-on condition" signal missing.  
Note:  
The "Internal switch-on condition" signal is generated from the monitoring signal
r0267.7 "Flow OK group signal".  
The "Switching on inhibited" signal is generated from the signal r0267.15 "Additional
fault present" or p6576 "Contactor safety circuit" interrupted.

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the wiring between the cooling unit and the input terminal (Terminal Module).  
- Check the cooling unit.  
- Check the external control device of the cooling unit.  
- The "switching on inhibited" for the converter when a differential pressure alarm,
RKA alarm, or temperature alarm is output and, in turn, the time overflow can be canceled
via p0276.  
See also: p0260, p0261, p0262, p0266, r0267, p6063, r6064

### A49169 (N) Cooling unit: Conductivity has exceeded alarm threshold 2

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
-

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
The conductivity of the cooling liquid has exceeded the selected alarm threshold 2
(p0269[2]).  
The ion exchanger is switched on (r0265.5).  
The pumps for the cooling liquid are switched on or remain switched on.  
The cooling unit is automatically switched on.  
Note:  
The threshold cannot be set higher than the fault threshold specified in the equipment
description.  
See also: r0265, r0267, r6033

**Remedy:**
  
Check the device to de-ionize the cooling liquid.

Reaction upon N:  
 NONE

Acknowl. upon N:  
NONE

### A49170 Cooling unit: Alarm has occurred

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
The control device of the cooling unit signals an alarm or did not receive the feedback
signal "cooling unit is switched on" or "cooling unit is ready" within the monitoring
time. The alarm value is sent the first time the alarm occurs.  
Alarm value (r2124, interpret hexadecimal):  
Bit 0: Group fault "RKA alarm" (p0266[2]) present.  
Bit 3: Timeout RKA in the sequence control. Feedback signal "Switched on" (p0266[0])
missing.  
Bit 4: Timeout RKA in the sequence control. Feedback signal "Ready" (p0266[1]) missing.  
See also: p0266 (Cooling unit feedback signals signal source)

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the wiring between the cooling unit and the input terminal (Terminal Module).  
- Check the external control device for the cooling unit.

### A49171 (N) Cooling unit: Conductivity has exceeded the alarm threshold

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Message value:**
  
-

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
The conductivity of the cooling liquid has exceeded the selected alarm threshold (p0269[1]).  
The ion exchanger is switched on (r0265.5).  
The pumps for the cooling liquid are switched on or remain switched on.  
The cooling unit is automatically switched on.  
Note:  
The threshold cannot be set higher than the fault threshold specified in the equipment
description.  
See also: r0268 (Cooling unit cooling liquid conductivity actual value), p0276

**Remedy:**
  
Check the device to de-ionize the cooling liquid.

Reaction upon N:  
 NONE

Acknowl. upon N:  
NONE

### A49172 Cooling unit: Conductivity actual value is not valid

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
-

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
When monitoring the conductivity of the cooling liquid, there is a fault in the wiring
or in the sensor.

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the wiring between the cooling unit and the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
- Check the function of the sensor to measure the conductivity.

### A49173 Cooling unit: Cooling liquid temperature has exceeded the alarm threshold

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, BMM2C, VECTORDM, VECTORM2C, VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
-

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
The cooling liquid intake temperature has exceeded the specified alarm threshold.  
Note:  
The value for the alarm threshold depends on the power unit (hardware description
data, e.g. 42 ... 50 °C).

**Remedy:**
  
Check the cooling system and the ambient conditions.

### A49174 Cooling unit: Cooling liquid temperature has fallen below the alarm threshold

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
-

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
The cooling liquid intake temperature has fallen below the permanently set alarm threshold
of 5 °C.  
This is the reason that the cooling unit is automatically switched on and prevents
the cooling liquid from freezing.

**Remedy:**
  
Check the ambient temperature.

### A49175 Cooling unit: The pressure monitoring has responded

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
The differential pressure has either not reached the alarm threshold and/or has fallen
below the alarm threshold or a pressure actual value has exceeded the permissible
maximum value.  
If the cooling unit is configured appropriately, the alarm prevents a converter pulse
enable signal during ramp-up.  
The alarm value is sent the first time the alarm occurs.  
The actual alarm value can be output from the ring buffer starting at r7100.  
Alarm value (r2124, interpret hexadecimal):  
Bit 0: Differential pressure has fallen below the alarm threshold (subsystem 1).  
Bit 1: One pressure actual value at the intake is too high (subsystem 1).  
Bit 2: One pressure actual value at the outlet is too high (subsystem 1).  
Bit 4: Differential pressure has fallen below the alarm threshold (subsystem 2).  
Bit 5: One pressure actual value at the intake is too high (subsystem 2).  
Bit 6: One pressure actual value at the outlet is too high (subsystem 2).

**Remedy:**
  
Check the cooling system and the ambient conditions.  
Notice:  
The switching on inhibited for the converter can be canceled via p0276.  
See also: p0276

### A49176 Cooling unit: Leakage monitor has detected leak

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, BMM2C

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
-

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
A leakage monitor has detected a leak in the liquid cooling circuit.  
Note:  
If configured appropriately at standstill, the alarm prevents a cooling unit enable
signal.

**Remedy:**
  
Check the piping of the cooling unit.  
Note:  
When the cooling unit control is appropriately parameterized, this alarm prevents
the drive converter from being switched on.  
See also: p0276

### A49178 (F) Cooling unit: Actual pressure sensing, wire breakage monitoring has responded

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.6

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
A wire breakage has been detected at a pressure sensor.  
Alarm value (r2124, interpret hexadecimal):  
Bit 0: Wire breakage, liquid pressure intake (subsystem 1).  
Bit 1: Wire breakage, liquid pressure outlet (subsystem 1).  
Bit 4: Wire breakage, liquid pressure intake (subsystem 2).  
Bit 5: Wire breakage, liquid pressure outlet (subsystem 2).  
Note:  
The message value is sent the first time the message occurs.  
The actual message value can be read out from the ring buffer starting at r7100.

**Remedy:**
  
Contact the manufacturer.

Reaction upon F:  
Vector: OFF1 (OFF2, OFF3)  
Chopper: OFF2  
Infeed: OFF1 (OFF2)

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY

### A49179 Cooling unit: Water flow has fallen below the alarm threshold

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
-

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
The flow of the cooling liquid has exceeded the selected alarm threshold (p6296[1]).

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the cooling liquid lines.  
- Check the pumps.

### A49180 (F) Cooling unit liquid flow actual value invalid

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

4.4

**Message value:**
  
-

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
The cooling liquid flow monitoring has identified a fault in the wiring or in the
sensor.

- Check the wiring between the cooling unit and the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
- Check the function of the sensor to measure the flow.

 NONE (OFF2)

### A49181 (F) Cooling unit: temperature actual value is not valid

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Message value:**
  
-

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
There is a fault in the wiring or in the temperature sensor for the cooling liquid
temperature monitoring.

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the wiring between the cooling unit and the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).  
- Check the function of the temperature sensor.

Reaction upon F:  
 NONE

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY

### A49190 Modbus topology error

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** Power Module | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
Components were not found in the Modbus topology.  
Alarm value (r2124, interpret decimal):  
Modbus address of the participant.

**Remedy:**
  
Check the connected Modbus communication to the slaves at the PSA X92 as well as the
connected Modbus participants.  
Note:  
PSA: Power Stack Adapter

### F49260 (A) Selectivity module SITOP PSE200U telegram error

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Message value:**
  
Info. 1: %1, info. 2: %2

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** Control Unit (CU) | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
Vector: OFF2 (OFF1)  
Chopper: OFF2 (NONE)  
Infeed: OFF2 (OFF1)

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The telegram evaluation for selectivity module SITOP PSE200U has an error condition.  
Info 1:  
1: the signal at binector input p17570 is permanently zero.  
2: the signal at binector input p17570 is permanently one.  
3: no valid telegram at binector input p17570 within the tolerance time (p17568[6]).  
4: telegram at binector input p17570 cannot be evaluated.  
Info 2:  
Index of binector input p17570.  
Note regarding the message value:  
The individual information is coded as follows in the message value (r0949/r2124):  
yyyyxxxx hex: yyyy = info. 1, xxxx = info. 2  
See also: p17570 (SITOP PSE200U terminal S pulses signal source)

**Remedy:**
  
Check that selectivity module SITOP PSE200U is functioning correctly.  
Note:  
The signal is read-in via the signal source of binector input p17570.

Reaction upon A:  
 NONE

Acknowl. upon A:  
NONE

VECTORSL

Infeed: OFF2 (OFF1)

overcurrent, overvoltage, undervoltage).

See also: p17570 (SITOP PSE200U terminal S pulses signal source)

**Remedy:**

 NONE

Acknowl. upon A:  
NONE

### F49300 Upgrade canceled

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Hardware/software error (1)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** Control Unit (CU) | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
Upgrading the firmware for the double submodule (DSM) or Power Module Interfaces (PMI)
has been canceled.  
Fault value (r0949, interpret decimal):  
The value indicates at which point the upgrade operation was canceled.  
1 ... 4:  
Canceled while preparing for the upgrade.  
5 ... 100:  
Canceled while the upgrade operation was running.  
See also: r17431 (DSM upgrade progress display)

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the double submodule (DSM) or power Module Interfaces (PMI).  
- Check the firmware version overview of the Power Stack Adapter (PSA).

### F49900 Sequence control timeout

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Internal (DRIVE-CLiQ) communication error (12)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The sequence control has been timed out.  
Fault value (r0949, interpret decimal):  
bbbx dec  
bbb: Corresponds to the state of the sequence control (r3402).  
x: Additional auxiliaries with missing feedback signal.  
3100: Timeout in the motor disconnector state.  
320x: Timeout in the additional auxiliaries state.  
x = 1: auxiliary 0 feedback signal missing or not switched on (r6594.0 = 0).  
x = 2: auxiliary 1 feedback signal missing or not switched on (r6594.1 = 0).  
x = 4: auxiliary 2 feedback signal missing or not switched on (r6594.2 = 0).  
3400: Timeout in the state motor side switch 1.  
3700: Timeout in the excitation state.

**Remedy:**
  
Check the feedback signals.

### N49904 (F, A) Sequence control auxiliaries feedback signal missing

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Auxiliary unit faulted (20)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
In the sequence control, the feedback signal from the additional auxiliaries is missing.  
Fault value (r0949, interpret hexadecimal):  
0001: auxiliary 0 feedback signal missing or not switched on (r6594.0 = 0).  
0002: auxiliary 1 feedback signal missing or not switched on (r6594.1 = 0).  
0004: auxiliary 2 feedback signal missing or not switched on (r6594.2 = 0).  
See also: p6593 (Sequence control auxiliaries feedback signal source), r6594 (Seq_ctrl auxiliaries
status word)

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the auxiliaries.  
- Check the wiring of the auxiliaries.  
- Check the setting of the parameter for the feedback signal of the auxiliaries.  
See also: p6593 (Sequence control auxiliaries feedback signal source)

Reaction upon F:  
Vector: NONE (OFF1, OFF2, OFF3)  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: NONE (OFF1, OFF2)

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY

Reaction upon A:  
 NONE

Acknowl. upon A:  
NONE

Vector: OFF1 (NONE, OFF1_DELAYED, OFF2, OFF3)  
Chopper: OFF2 (NONE)  
Infeed: OFF1 (NONE, OFF1_DELAYED, OFF2)

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY

### F49955 Sign­of­life monitoring fault

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
External measured value / signal state outside the permissible range (16)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
Vector: OFF2  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: OFF2

**Acknowledge:**
  
IMMEDIATELY

**Cause:**
  
The sign-of-life monitoring has detected a fault.  
Fault value (r0949, interpret decimal):  
Block number (instance):  
See also: p17983 (Sign-of-life monitoring mode), p17984 (Sign-of-life monitoring tolerance counter),
p17985 (Sign-of-life monitoring cycle), p17986 (Sign-of-life monitoring input), r17987
(Sign-of-life monitoring output)

**Remedy:**
  
- Observe bus propagation times – and when parameterizing, take into account the sign-of-life
monitoring.  
- Check the monitored connection for disturbances.

### A49998 Recorder trigger event occurred

**Drive object:**
  
A_INFM2C, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, BMM2C, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, VECTORGL, VECTORM2C, VECTORMV,
VECTORSL

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Hardware/software error (1)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** GLOBAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
A recorder trigger event has occurred. The data are then written to the memory card,
specifying the event number.  
Alarm value (r2124, interpret decimal):  
Event number.  
See also: r6991, r6992, p6993, p6994, p6996, r6997, p6998, p6999

**Remedy:**
  
Not necessary.  
This message disappears automatically.

### A50001 (F) PN/COMM BOARD: Configuration error

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Communication error to the higher-level control system (9)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
CBE20:  
A PROFINET controller attempts to establish a connection using an incorrect configuring
telegram. The "Shared Device" function has been activated (p8829 = 2).  
Alarm value (r2124, interpret decimal):  
10: A CPU sends a PROFIsafe telegram.  
11: F CPU sends a PZD telegram.  
12: F CPU without an A CPU.  
13: F CPU with more PROFIsafe subslots than activated with p9601.3.  
14: F CPU with fewer PROFIsafe subslots than activated with p9601.3.  
15: PROFIsafe telegram of the F-CPU does not match the setting in p60022.

**Remedy:**
  
CBE20:  
Check the configuration of the PROFINET controllers as well as the p8829 and p9601.3
setting.

Reaction upon F:  
Vector: NONE (OFF1, OFF2, OFF3)  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: NONE (OFF1, OFF2)

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**

Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: NONE (OFF1, OFF2)

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY

### A50003 (F) COMM BOARD: Alarm 3

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
Info. 1: %1, info. 2: %2

**Message class:**
  
Communication error to the higher-level control system (9)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
CBE20 SINAMICS Link:  
A specific telegram word (receive) is being used twice.  
Alarm value (r2124, interpret hexadecimal):  
yyyyxxxx hex: yyyy = info. 1, xxxx = info. 2  
Info. 1 (decimal) = Address of sender  
Info. 2 (decimal) = Receive telegram word  
See also: p8870 (SINAMICS Link PZD receive word), p8872 (SINAMICS Link PZD receive address)

**Remedy:**
  
CBE20 SINAMICS Link:  
Correct the parameter assignment.

Reaction upon F:  
Vector: NONE (OFF1, OFF2, OFF3)  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: NONE (OFF1, OFF2)

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY

### A50004 (F) COMM BOARD: Alarm 4

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
Info. 1: %1, info. 2: %2

**Message class:**
  
Communication error to the higher-level control system (9)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
CBE20 SINAMICS Link:  
- Telegram word (receive) and address of sender inconsistent. Both values have to
be either equal to zero or not equal to zero.  
- Address of the sender > maximum project address.  
Alarm value (r2124, interpret hexadecimal):  
yyyyxxxx hex: yyyy = info. 1, xxxx = info. 2  
Info. 1 (decimal) = Drive object number from p8870, p8872  
Info. 2 (decimal) = Index from p8870, p8872  
See also: p8811, p8870, p8872

**Remedy:**
  
In the case of CBE20 SINAMICS Link:  
Correct the parameter assignment.

Reaction upon F:  
Vector: NONE (OFF1, OFF2, OFF3)  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: NONE (OFF1, OFF2)

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY

### A50005 (F) COMM BOARD: Alarm 5

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Communication error to the higher-level control system (9)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
CBE20 SINAMICS Link:  
Sender not found on SINAMICS Link.  
Alarm value (r2124, interpret decimal):  
0: synchronization to the bus clock cycle unsuccessful.  
1 ... 64: address of the sender that was not found.  
See also: p8872 (SINAMICS Link PZD receive address)

**Remedy:**
  
CBE20 SINAMICS Link:  
Check the connection to the sender.  
Set parameters p8811, p8812[1] to identical values for all participants/nodes.  
Check parameter p8836 for all participants.  
See also: p8811 (SINAMICS Link project selection), p8812 (SINAMICS Link clock cycle settings),
p8836 (SINAMICS link node address)

Reaction upon F:  
Vector: NONE (OFF1, OFF2, OFF3)  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: NONE (OFF1, OFF2)

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY

### A50006 (F) COMM BOARD: Alarm 6

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
Info. 1: %1, info. 2: %2

**Message class:**
  
Communication error to the higher-level control system (9)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
CBE20 SINAMICS Link:  
The parameter assignment indicates that the sender and the receiver are one and the
same. This is not permitted.  
Alarm value (r2124, interpret hexadecimal):  
yyyyxxxx hex: yyyy = info. 1, xxxx = info. 2  
Info. 1 (decimal) = Drive object number from p8872  
Info. 2 (decimal) = Index from p8872  
See also: p8836 (SINAMICS link node address), p8872 (SINAMICS Link PZD receive address)

**Remedy:**
  
In the case of CBE20 SINAMICS Link:  
Correct the parameter assignment. All p8872[index] must be set to a value not equal
to p8836.

Reaction upon F:  
Vector: NONE (OFF1, OFF2, OFF3)  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: NONE (OFF1, OFF2)

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY

### A50010 (F) PNCOMM BOARD: Consistency error affecting adjustable parameters

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Communication error to the higher-level control system (9)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
A consistency error was detected when activating the configuration (p8945) for the
Communication Board Ethernet 20 (CBE20).  
Alarm value (r2124, interpret decimal):  
0: General consistency error  
1: Error in the IP configuration (IP address, subnet mask or standard gateway).  
2: Error in the station names.  
3: DHCP was not able to be activated, as a cyclic PROFINET connection already exists.  
4: A cyclic PROFINET connection is not possible as DHCP is activated.  
Note:  
For all alarm values, the following applies: currently set configuration has not been
activated.  
DHCP: Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol  
See also: p8940 (CBExx name of station), p8941 (CBExx IP address), p8942 (CBExx default gateway),
p8943 (CBExx subnet mask), p8944 (CBExx DHCP mode)

**Remedy:**
  
- Check the required interface configuration (p8940 and following), correct if necessary,
and activate (p8945).  
or  
- Reinitialize the station using the "Edit Ethernet node" screen form (e.g. with STARTER
commissioning tool).  
See also: p8945 (CBExx activate configuration of interfaces)

Reaction upon F:  
Vector: NONE (OFF1, OFF2, OFF3)  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: NONE (OFF1, OFF2)

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY

### A50011 (F) EtherNetIP/COMM BOARD: configuration error

**Drive object:**
  
All objects

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Message value:**
  
%1

**Message class:**
  
Communication error to the higher-level control system (9)

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **Component:** None | **Propagation:** LOCAL |

  

**Reaction:**
  
 NONE

**Acknowledge:**
  
NONE

**Cause:**
  
CBE20 EtherNet/IP:  
An EtherNet/IP controller attempts to establish a connection using an incorrect configuring
telegram.  
The telegram length set in the controller does not match the parameterization in the
drive device.

**Remedy:**
  
Check the set telegram length.  
Note:  
PZD interface 1:  
For p0922 not equal to 999, then the length of the selected telegram applies.  
For p0922 = 999, the maximum interconnected PZD (r2067) applies.  
PZD interface 2:  
The maximum interconnected PZD (r8867) applies.  
See also: p0922, r2067, r8867

Reaction upon F:  
Vector: NONE (OFF1, OFF2, OFF3)  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: NONE (OFF1, OFF2)

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY

Vector: NONE (OFF1, OFF2, OFF3)  
Chopper: NONE (OFF2)  
Infeed: NONE (OFF1, OFF2)

Acknowl. upon F:  
IMMEDIATELY
